title: bitwarden: cached auth client never refreshes; token expiry silently breaks long-running processes
description: BitwardenBackend uses OnceCell to cache the authenticated client (lib:204-207). Once auth succeeds the client is cached for the lifetime of the struct. If BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN is rotated externally (Bitwarden SM tokens typically expire), subsequent calls fail with opaque API errors. Users running long-lived processes will file issues believing the backend is broken. Fix: document that BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN is read once and callers must recreate the backend on token rotation, or add a refresh mechanism.
